subject: Cloud Computing
title: GCP and Open Source Strategy
description: A 60-second lesson on GCP and Open Source Strategy.

hook.text: Google open-sourced Kubernetes, TensorFlow, and Go. Giving away crown jewels sounds crazy — unless it's strategic.

buildup.text: Open-source projects attract developers. Developers choose platforms. Platforms drive cloud revenue.

discovery.text: Kubernetes became the industry standard. Google's managed version, GKE, is widely considered the best.

twist.text: But AWS and Azure offer Kubernetes too. Google's open-source gifts benefited competitors as much as itself.

climax.text: Google's open-source strategy built goodwill and mindshare — but didn't translate into market share fast enough.

punchline.text: Google gave the cloud its best tools. Others profited more.

quiz.question: Why did Google open-source Kubernetes?
options: To attract developers and drive adoption of Google Cloud's managed Kubernetes service
Google was legally required to release all internal tools as open source
Kubernetes was too expensive for Google to maintain internally
